compiled_state.title: GREEN: implement deep-tier dispatch in ci-llm-review-runner.sh
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
Replace the deep-tier fallback stub in plugins/dso/scripts/ci-llm-review-runner.sh (lines 60-66) with a real implementation that satisfies the story's Done Definitions and the RED tests from Task f3b6-83cd.

Implementation contract:
- On selected_tier=deep: dispatch 3 parallel curl calls (background subshells), one each for code-reviewer-deep-correctness.md, code-reviewer-deep-verification.md, code-reviewer-deep-hygiene.md (agent files at $_PLUGIN_ROOT/agents/).
- Each specialist call: same curl pattern as lines 86-91; LLM response parsed and extracted as in lines 93-133; written to slot file: ${WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR}/reviewer-findings-{correctness,verification,hygiene}.json.
- After all 3 background jobs complete (wait), check all 3 slot files exist and contain valid JSON; if any is missing or malformed, print an error to stderr and exit non-zero (fail-closed, no arch call).
- Dispatch 4th sequential curl call using code-reviewer-deep-arch.md; arch system prompt receives the 3 slot file contents embedded in the user message (so arch agent can synthesize); arch response is passed to write-reviewer-findings.sh and written as reviewer-findings.json (sole final writer — no direct writes from this script).
- Pass --review-tier deep --selected-tier deep to write-reviewer-findings.sh.
- Existing light/standard paths are unchanged.
- Parallel curl calls: use & + wait; no external parallel utilities required.
- Rate limit handling: rely on curl -sf -m 30 --retry 3 --retry-delay 5 (add --retry flags to deep-tier calls); document that check-usage.sh is not called in CI context (it requires Claude OAuth session, not available in CI).

Done criteria: all 4 RED tests from Task f3b6-83cd pass; all 22 pre-existing tests in the file continue to pass; bash -n passes on the modified script.
